notes: L11 Orientation (act 66) 'Fingerpicking Fundamentals: Why Your Hands Should Do Different Jobs', 1020.19s, 7 cues. Uses guitar.py (StringSet, region_brace, pima_row, pluck). READ start/mid/end each cue; scenes accumulate + hold so mid==end (no transient/fading labels). 1 layout fix at smoke stage (Cue01 pima-row overlapping role text -> stacked roles below row); -ql pass clean. No formulas (rhythmic topic).

frames.notes: [start reveal] bass strings 6/5/4 tinted amber under brace 'thumb: bass pulse' vs treble 3/2/1 blue brace 'fingers: melody'; 'stop competing for the same job'. Strings thicker toward bass. Legible, in-frame.

[mid, full] bass strings 6/5/4 tinted amber under brace 'thumb: bass pulse' vs treble 3/2/1 blue brace 'fingers: melody'; 'stop competing for the same job'. Strings thicker toward bass. Legible, in-frame.

[start reveal] object text 'a repeating picking pattern, split between bass and melody strings' + 'a small band in one hand'; PIMA chips p(amber)/i(blue)/m(green)/a(violet); roles 'p -> bass player: keeps the floor steady', 'i·m·a -> upper voices: rhythm & color'. No overlap after fix.
